methodology_notes: Values are the annual violent crime rates per 100,000 residents reported in Table 6 of NCSBI's Crime in North Carolina annual summary reports.
comparability_notes: North Carolina shifted local reporting to NIBRS on Jan. 1, 2018, but NCSBI states that it converts NIBRS data back into Summary Reporting System format for long-term trend analysis. Wayne County's 2018 county rate is specifically flagged in the 2019 annual summary as partial-year data for over 50 percent of the county population.
caveats: This first real county series is rate-based; raw county violent crime counts still need to be added.
2018 should be interpreted cautiously because the 2019 annual summary flags partial-year data for over 50 percent of Wayne County's population.
Additional pre-2007 county annual summary reports should be added next from the historical archive.

series_breaks.label: Reporting transition to NIBRS
series_breaks.note: NCSBI says it converts NIBRS data back into SRS format for long-term trend analysis, but some agencies may still see small differences in rates.

series_breaks.label: Wayne County partial-year flag
series_breaks.note: The 2019 annual summary marks Wayne County's 2018 county rate as partial-year data for over 50 percent of the county population.
